claim: Fourth MCC measurement. Same 20-gene balanced panel at scale=0.25 (5x larger than v0-v2) with Rust backend + calibration + expanded pool set (now includes TOTAL_COMPLEXES, FOLDED_PROTEINS, UNFOLDED_PROTEINS, FOLDED_FRACTION, BOUND_PROTEINS). Result: **MCC=0.229** (TP=1, FP=0, TN=10, FN=9). Identical to v1/v2 despite the 5x scale increase. pgi's F6P deviation grows from -11%% at scale=0.05 to -15%% at scale=0.25, confirming the signal is real and scales. But no other essential gene produces a deviation exceeding the safety-factor * noise-floor threshold at 0.5s bio-time.
caveats: Conclusively establishes that the short-window (<=0.5 s bio-time) essentiality-detection ceiling is architectural, not threshold-tuning. MCC stays at 0.229 across scale={0.05, 0.10, 0.25} - only pgi (central glycolysis) ever trips.
Attempted scale=0.5 (with Rust + 4-worker parallel + 5-gene calibration) but timed out at 540 s wall - too expensive for interactive Bash calls.
Non-metabolic pools TOTAL_COMPLEXES and BOUND_PROTEINS have noise-floors of 0.27-0.40 at scale=0.25; threshold (safety_factor=2.5) therefore > 0.6, which no essential-gene KO reaches in 0.5 s.

Break through requires non-short-window runs. Either: (a) t_end on the order of seconds-to-minutes bio-time at moderate scale (cluster job, many hours), or (b) event-count-based detection (fraction of catalysis events for downstream reactions - zero in the KO case, high in WT).
